sections.instruction: Questions 1-4: Complete the table below. Write ONE WORD ONLY. Questions 5 and 6: Choose TWO letters A-E. Questions 7-10: Complete the table below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER.
sections.passage_or_context:
HEALTH CENTRES
Talks for patients at Shore Lane Health Centre

questions.content: Which TWO of the following are offered free of charge at Shore Lane Health Centre?

sections.instruction: Questions 11-13: Label the diagram below. Choose THREE answers from the box and write the correct letters A-E. Questions 14-18: Where can each of the following items be found? Choose FIVE answers from the options and write the correct letter A-G. Questions 19 and 20: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD OR A NUMBER.

options: A in box on washing machine
D next to window in living room
E on shelf of back door

sections.instruction: Questions 21 and 22: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 23-25: Complete the sentences below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer. Questions 26-30: Answer the questions below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.content:
21. In her home country, Kira had
22. To succeed with assignments, Kira had to
options: 21B done two years of a course

sections.instruction: Questions 31-36: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 37-40: Complete the table below. Write ONE WORD ONLY.

questions.content:
31. What led the group to choose their topic?
32. The exact proportion of land devoted to private gardens was confirmed by
33. The group asked garden owners to
34. The group made their observations in gardens
35. The group did extensive reading on
36. The speaker focuses on three animal species because
options: 31A They were concerned about the decline of one species
31B They were interested in the effects of city growth
31C They wanted to investigate a recent phenomenon
33B keep a record of animals they saw
33C get in contact when they saw a rare species
34A which had a large number of animal species
34B which they considered to be representative
34C which had stable populations of rare animals
36A a lot of data has been obtained about them
36B the group were most interested in them
